resolution_rule: Every open sink must exit by either an explicit conditional closure theorem under named hypotheses or a machine-checkable countermodel schema.

closure_exit.minimal_form: For the intended admissible domain D, prove exists epsilon > 0 such that every admissible fiber mass is at least epsilon.

countermodel_exit.minimal_form: Construct an admissible sequence whose positive fiber masses tend to 0.

closure_exit.minimal_form: Embed the finite positive support domain into the intended admissible domain while preserving the uniform floor.
closure_exit.downstream_effect: Strengthens finite-support restricted Chronos-RR toward admissible restricted Chronos-RR.

countermodel_exit.minimal_form: Show a finite-support uniform floor need not survive the intended admissible-domain lift.
countermodel_exit.downstream_effect: Forces the finite-support route to remain isolated.

closure_exit.minimal_form: For each remaining sink, give an explicit theorem statement with named hypotheses and verifier coverage.

countermodel_exit.minimal_form: For each remaining sink, give a concrete model showing why the unrestricted route fails.
countermodel_exit.downstream_effect: Converts vague openness into certified obstruction.
